asr_es: Gracias señor presidente. Hoy también, como Día Internacional de la Mujer, yo querría hacer un llamamiento a este plenario. No voy a hablar ni de la discriminación, porque ya lo han hablado anteriores compañeras mías, pero sí que creo que hay un problema contra la violencia de género en todo el mundo y especialmente en mi Estado, y creo que tanto las instituciones como la sociedad tienen que tomar conciencia de que esto se tiene que solucionar. Y yo pediría a este plenario, por eso he pedido la palabra, un minuto de silencio por las 13 mujeres que ya llevan muertas en España, más el resto que han sido asesinadas en todo el mundo. Gracias.
hypothesis_en: Thank you, Mr President. Today too, on International Women's Day, I would like to make an appeal to this plenary. I will not even talk about discrimination, because my previous colleagues have already spoken about it, but I do believe that there is a problem against gender violence all over the world and especially in my state, and I think that both institutions and society have to become aware that this has to be solved. And I would ask this plenary, which is why I have asked for the floor, for a minute of silence for the 13 women who have already been killed in Spain, plus the rest who have been killed all over the world. Thank you.
reference_en: thank you very much (1.57) President today is International Women's Day (2.21) and I'd like to address (1.06) the plenary not talking about discrimination because other people have already talked about that (1.16) but I do feel there is one issue (1.75) gender violence in particular in my country and we see institutions and society (1.2) needing to become more aware of how we do need to find a solution to the issue of violence and I've asked for one minute's silence to be respected for the thirteen women who have died in Spain (1.28) in addition to those who have been murdered throughout the world thank you very much
